skill.name: symptom-checker-triage
skill.description: Suggest triage levels (Emergency, Urgent, Outpatient) based on red flag symptoms using a rule-based engine. For AI-assisted decision support only — not a substitute for professional medical diagnosis.
skill.summary: Rule-based triage tool that classifies symptom descriptions into Emergency, Urgent, or Outpatient levels using clinical red flag detection.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 50 static analyzer findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (31 instances) target non-existent crypto code — the Python script uses no cryptographic operations. 'System reconnaissance' findings (4 instances) target medical symptom data definitions and key strengths text. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings (14 instances) target bash code blocks in SKILL.md that are documentation examples, not executed commands. The single env_access finding is os.environ.get('TERM') for terminal color support, which is benign. The previous auditor evaluation (score 87/100, Production Ready grade) confirms this skill is safe. The primary risk factor is external_commands (SKILL.md instructs AI to run python scripts/main.py), which is expected behavior for this skill type and targets well-scoped, safe code.

medium_findings.title: External Script Execution Instructed in Documentation
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash commands that instruct the AI to execute python scripts/main.py with user-provided symptom text. While the script itself is safe (no network calls, no filesystem writes, no eval/exec operations), external command execution is a detected pattern that warrants awareness.

content.user_title: Check Symptoms and Get Triage Recommendations
content.value_statement: Patients and healthcare workers need quick clarity on symptom urgency. This skill analyzes symptom descriptions against clinical red flag criteria to suggest a triage level with supporting rationale.
actual_capabilities: Analyze symptom descriptions and classify into Emergency, Urgent, or Outpatient levels
Identify red flag symptoms across cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological, and gastrointestinal systems
Generate structured triage reports with confidence scores and clinical rationale
Support interactive mode for iterative symptom input and assessment
Output results in JSON format for integration with documentation systems
Expand symptom synonyms to improve matching accuracy for common medical terms
limitations: Does not provide specific diagnoses or treatment recommendations
Rule-based engine cannot recognize symptoms outside defined keyword lists
Only supports English and Chinese symptom descriptions
Not validated for pediatric, geriatric, or pregnancy-specific triage protocols

use_cases.title: Emergency Department Pre-Screening
use_cases.description: Triage nurses pre-screen incoming patient symptom descriptions to quickly identify life-threatening red flags before full clinical assessment.
use_cases.target_user: Triage nurses and emergency medical staff

use_cases.title: Telemedicine Intake Triage
use_cases.description: Telehealth providers assess patient-reported symptoms during virtual visits to determine appropriate care level and referral urgency.
use_cases.target_user: Telemedicine practitioners and virtual care providers

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Symptom Classification
use_cases.description: Researchers categorize patient symptom reports into standardized urgency levels for clinical studies and retrospective data analysis.
use_cases.target_user: Clinical researchers and medical data analysts

prompt_templates.title: Simple Symptom Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick triage assessment of a single symptom description

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Symptom Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Triage these symptoms: {symptom list}. Identify all red flags and provide confidence level for each finding.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed assessment of multiple symptoms with supporting rationale

prompt_templates.title: Batch Symptom Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process each symptom set from this list and output triage results with emergency, urgent, or outpatient levels. Include confidence scores and department recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch triage processing for clinical intake workflows

prompt_templates.title: Custom Protocol Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the symptom checker on this input and map the output to our internal triage categories. Include all red flags and the medical disclaimer.
prompt_templates.scenario: Integration with existing clinical protocols and documentation workflows

output_examples.input: Patient reports chest pain radiating to left arm with shortness of breath
output_examples.output: Emergency level triage -- identified red flags: Chest Pain, Dyspnea. Recommendation: Proceed to emergency immediately. Confidence: 95%. This is AI-assisted advice only and cannot replace professional medical diagnosis.

output_examples.input: Mild headache and low-grade fever for two days, no other symptoms
output_examples.output: Outpatient level triage -- identified symptoms: Mild Headache, Low-grade Fever. No red flags detected. Recommendation: Schedule outpatient appointment. Seek urgent care if symptoms worsen. This is AI-assisted advice only.

best_practices: Always include the medical disclaimer that triage suggestions are AI-assisted and not a substitute for professional medical evaluation
Provide complete symptom descriptions including duration, severity, and associated symptoms for accurate triage
Verify triage results through clinical review before making care decisions for actual patients
anti_patterns: Using the tool to diagnose specific diseases or prescribe medications instead of triaging symptoms
Relying solely on tool output without professional medical judgment for emergency care decisions
Inputting incomplete or vague symptom descriptions that may miss critical red flags

faq.question: What triage levels does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill classifies symptoms into three levels: Emergency (life-threatening), Urgent (serious but not immediately fatal), and Outpatient (non-urgent).

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose my medical condition?
faq.answer: No. This skill only suggests triage levels based on symptom keyword matching. It does not provide diagnoses or treatment recommendations.

faq.question: What languages does the symptom checker support?
faq.answer: The skill accepts symptom descriptions in English and Chinese.

faq.question: How does red flag detection work?
faq.answer: The skill uses a rule-based engine that matches symptom keywords against predefined clinical red flag categories including cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological, and gastrointestinal systems.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for pediatric or geriatric patients?
faq.answer: The skill has limited pediatric and geriatric-specific triage rules. Always consult a healthcare professional for these populations.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my electronic health record system?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON output format that can be integrated into documentation workflows. It is not certified for clinical use.
